_comment: Reference source config for the economy brief's FX values. Each KEY is a metric; its VALUE is the source LADDER (tried top to bottom). The engine returns the first rung that yields a value. Authoritative rung first (ECB), key-free fallback below (Frankfurter, also ECB-backed). Economy owner: extend/replace these rungs and add DXY etc. Both rungs expose a real sample date so the value isn't flagged date_inferred. Avoid sources that need an API key (e.g. exchangerate.host now does) — the engine will report a parse miss and step down, but a key-gated rung is a dead rung.
_comment_dxy: DXY (US-Dollar-Index) is a basket index, not an FX pair, so the key-free pair APIs above don't carry it. The rung below scrapes the TradingEconomics currency page the brief already cites; if its markup shifts the regex misses and the engine reports it (no fabrication). DXY has no clean key-free JSON source — if this rung fails, source DXY by hand for the day. The fx-snapshot cross-check does NOT depend on DXY, so a missing DXY never blocks the EUR/BRL consistency check.
